title: file put(): secret bytes copied to plain Vec<u8> before spawn_blocking
description:
In secretx-file/src/lib.rs put(): 'let bytes = value.as_bytes().to_vec()' creates a plain (non-Zeroizing) Vec<u8> holding the secret in plaintext. This violates the explicit project rule: 'Never copy inner bytes into a non-Zeroizing buffer inside any backend.' Fix: replace with 'let bytes = value.into_bytes()' (returns Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>). write_secret_file takes &[u8] so &bytes still works via Deref. Trivial change, zero behavior difference.

**Why:** The Zeroizing guarantee is the reason SecretValue exists. A contributor who copies this pattern in a new backend will also violate the rule.
**How to apply:** Check every put() impl for .to_vec() calls on secret bytes; require Zeroizing intermediates.
